free_text: Cross section for 165 degree backscattering of 2.0-9.0
A Zr-N/Cu/C sample was used for the cross section
measurement. A layer of Cu film was deposited on a
polished pyrolytic carbon substrate, which provided a
clear separation of the Zr-N signal from the substrate.
Thereafter a deposition layer of Zr-N was sputtered
onto the Cu layer.  The Zr signal was used as a
comparative signal for the determination of He-N cross
The results were applied to the analysis of nitrogen
contents implanted in the stainless steel by using
A standard Si(Au) surface barrier detector.
All data were taken from Sigma Data Base.
